intro: Galarhoeus rigidus, also known as Galla or Stomachache Plant, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the Asclepiadaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Galarhoeus rigidus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal preparations, Galarhoeus rigidus is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to treat stomachache, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal disorders.
